title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Independent Repository Trial
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a history-rewrite plan and verification graph with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

What must be established before implementation?

stimulus.title: Rewrite Verification failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Rewrite Verification condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Rewrite Verification output and hide the violated a history-rewrite plan and verification graph assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Rewrite Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
steps.why: Retrieving Rewrite Verification through the concrete a history-rewrite plan and verification graph case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
